title: Reorder List
description:
You're given `values`, the contents of a singly linked list listed from head to tail. Rearrange the nodes in place so that the sequence zig-zags inward from both ends at once: keep the original first node where it is, then place the original last node right after it, then the original second node, then the original second-to-last node, and so on, alternating front and back until every node has been placed exactly once. Return the resulting order of values.

**Example**

```
values = [1, 2, 3, 4]        ->  [1, 4, 2, 3]
values = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]     ->  [1, 5, 2, 4, 3]
```
starter_code:
def reorder_list(values):
    # values: list of node values in original head-to-tail order.
    # Return the values in the reordered (zig-zag from both ends) order.
    pass
hints: Build the list as real nodes, then find the middle with a slow/fast pointer so you can split it into a first half and a second half.
Reverse the second half in place (same pointer-flip technique as Reverse Linked List), then merge the two halves by alternately splicing a node from each.

solutions.name: Find middle, reverse second half, merge alternately
solutions.explanation: Build real nodes from values. Find the middle of the list using slow/fast pointers (slow ends at the start of the second half, using the convention that for even length the second half is the shorter or equal one). Split the list into two halves, reverse the second half with the standard iterative prev/curr pointer flip, then merge the two halves by alternately taking one node from the first half and one from the reversed second half. Finally read the values back out of the merged list.
solutions.code:
class _Node:
    __slots__ = ("val", "next")
    def __init__(self, val):
        self.val = val
        self.next = None


def _build(values):
    head = None
    tail = None
    for v in values:
        node = _Node(v)
        if head is None:
            head = tail = node
        else:
            tail.next = node
            tail = node
    return head


def _reverse(head):
    prev = None
    curr = head
    while curr is not None:
        nxt = curr.next
        curr.next = prev
        prev = curr
        curr = nxt
    return prev


def reorder_list(values):
    head = _build(values)
    if head is None or head.next is None:
        return values[:]

    slow = fast = head
    while fast.next is not None and fast.next.next is not None:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next

    second = slow.next
    slow.next = None
    second = _reverse(second)

    first = head
    while second is not None:
        first_next = first.next
        second_next = second.next
        first.next = second
        second.next = first_next
        first = first_next
        second = second_next

    out = []
    node = head
    while node is not None:
        out.append(node.val)
        node = node.next
    return out
solutions.complexity: Time O(n), Space O(1) (excluding the O(n) list construction)
